rats were given lightshock pairings on successive days at hr following training groups of rats received BL transection of the cerebellar peduncles BL lesions of the RN which receives most of the cerebellar efferents or bilateral lesions of the central nucleus of the amygdala control rats were sham operated at days T3 surgery the rats were tested for potentiated startle increased acoustic startle in the presence of the light previously paired with shock potentiated startle was blocked by lesions of the central nucleus of the amygdala transection of the cerebellar peduncles or lesions of the RN did not block potentiated startle a second experiment in which a visual prepulse test was used indicated that the blockade of potentiated startle observed in the animals with amygdala lesions could not be attributed to optic tract damage a third experiment demonstrated that the absence of potentiation in the animals with amygdala lesions was not simply due to a lowered startle level ceiling because these animals could show increased startle with increased stimulus intensity and with administration of strychnine a drug that increases startle taken together the results are consistent with the hypothesis that the amygdala is involved in fear conditioning because potentiated startle is a measure of CS fear

mainly present in the mitochondria the translocator protein tspo previously known as the IP benzodiazepine receptor is a small essential membrane protein involved in the translocation of cholesterol across mitochondrial CM a rate determining step in steroids biosynthesis we previously reported the structure of five fragments encompassing the five putative TMs and showed that each of these fragments constitutes an autonomous folding unit to further characterize the structural determinants responsible for helixhelix association of this membrane protein we now investigate the folding of double TM domains in various detergent micelles herein we present the successful biosynthesis of a double TM domain encompassing the last two CT helices tmtm for optimal production of this domain in escherichia coli the evaluation of various peptide constructs including tmtm fused to different purification tags or to solubilizing proteins was necessary the protocol of production of tmtm with more than purity is reported this domain was further characterized using circular dichroism and solution state nmr faruv circular dichroism studies indicate that the secondary structure of tmtm is highly helical when solubilized in various detergent micelles including ndodecylÎ²dmaltoside noctylÎ²dglucoside ndodecylphosphocholine dihexanoylsnglycerophosphocholine dhpc and palmitoylhydroxysnglycerophosphoracglycerol in addition the solubilization conditions of the domain were optimized for nmr experiments and preliminary analysis indicates that tmtm adopts a SD tertiary fold within the tmtmdhpc complex

the objective of the present study was to identify predictors of left atrial SEC sec or thrombus in patients with stroke with SR and left ventricular dysfunction of consecutive patients with stroke with sinus rhythm and reduced left ventricular ejection fractions efs or were examined ten patients presented with sec or thrombus the patients with sec or thrombus had larger left atrial diameters vs mm p smaller efs vs p and slower LAA laa flow velocities vs cms p multivariate analysis identified ef or and laa flow velocity or cms as predictors of sec or thrombus p patients with stroke with sinus rhythm and moderate to highgrade reduction of the left ventricular ef represent a risk group for a left atrial source of embolism and should undergo transesophageal echocardiography

gastric CA remains a leading cause of cancerrelated deaths in many parts of the world at present prevention seems to be the most effective means to reduce its the incidence and MR gastric atrophy is considered the first relevant step in the histogenesis of gastric adenocarcinoma however whether screening for and surveillance of atrophic gastritis is advisable is debated the prevalence and DP of CAG varies greatly from country to country being higher and mainly diffuse pangastritis or localized in those countries with a high gastric cancer incidence the only method available to detect gastric atrophy is histopathological examination of endoscopic specimens but there is no consensus about diagnosis SS gastric secretion may be a marker of gastric atrophy although it has high specificity but low sensitivity gastric atrophy is mainly related to chronic helicobacter pylori h pylori infection thus the only effective strategy for GC prevention is eradication of h pylori infection to arrest atrophy progression in selected populations in conclusion there is insufficient evidence to suggest screening for and surveillance of atrophic gastritis in the GA population however this strategy should be applied in countries with a high incidence of GC

using picosecond absorption spectroscopy it has been shown that in rhodobacter sphaeroides reaction centres the substitution of the primary quinone acceptor qa ubiquinone by other quinone species with redox potentials higher or lower than that of ubiquinone has essentially no modifying effect on the RC protein the molecular EC50 processes that accompany the localization and stabilization of a photoexcited electron on the intermediate acceptor bacteriopheophytin i are not affected although the subsequent transfer of the electron from i to qa is slowed down consequently this leads to a lower quantum efficiency of high rate of direct iqa reaction is normally due to the specificity of the primary quinone species and its IB site in the RC protein which provide optimum steric and chemical conditions for an ERP interaction between i and qa

examines the interaction of patient organisations with the national institute for clinical excellence nice during the first two years of its existence in particular it considers the intersection of two policy Az prominent in the labour governments health reformspatient participation and EBM data has been obtained from unstructured interviews with patientcarer representatives from nices committees and patientcarer CG with an interest in nices technology appraisals supplemented by observation of nices board and partners council meetings and analysis of documentary evidence the paper focuses on formal and informal involvement of patient groups in nices structures and appraisals process most interviewees felt that the patient voice had been strengthened in these areas though there was concern about the relative weights of patient and scientific evidence thus nice illustrates two paradoxes in labours policy objectivescentralisationparticipation and evidencebased medicinepatient perspectivewhich may become problematic

the aim of this T0 was to investigate growth and FH in young adults T3 therapy for malignant diseases final height and weight was studied in LTS lts of childhood CA aged years men women years after treatment for malignant diseases acute lymphoblastic leukemia lymphoma sarcoma malignant central nervous system cns tumours none of the lts had been treated with growth hormone gh a decrease in final height sds standard deviation score occurred in both lts of malignant cns tumours median height sds at diagnosis range to median FH sds range to p and lts of lymphoma p or leukemia p however only lts who received cranial p or craniospinal p irradiation xrt exhibited reduced final heights lts who had received xrt not involving the cns or had received no xrt at all presented no reduction in FH lts of cns tumours treated with high craniospinal xrt doses to gy reached lower p final heights when compared with lts of leukemia who received lower cranial xrt doses to gy FH sds correlated with chronological age at initiation of therapy p no correlation was found between the cumulative doses of applied chemotherapeutic agents and the final height of lts during followup lts developed an increase in weight for height index wfh which occurred independent of xrt in conclusion cranial and craniospinal xrt especially in young children with malignancies resulted in a decrease in final height sds as of lts of malignant cns tumours exhibited a final height sds below sd analysis of PIT function and substitution of gh after diagnosis of GHD should be considered for these patients at a young age others factors not directly related to xrt are responsible for the increased risk for obesity in lts of childhood CA

to directly activate specific gene expression the estrogen receptor er must bind to estrogen receptor response elements eres in the context of nucleosomes in order to investigate the interaction of the er with mononucleosomes we developed a mononucleosome gel shift CA a bp high TPS activity pprobe dna bp consensus ere with flanking regions separated by nucleotides from an artificial nucleosome positioning CS was prepared nuclear extracts from mcf cells or recombinant human eralpha were incubated with the labeled ere excess ere a retarded band was seen which was CR obliterated with excess ere confirming the specificity of IB this probe was then used to make reconstituted mononucleosomes by sequential dilution of a high salt histone preparadual cell transients was reduced by gap junction blockers use of the gap junction permeant tracer NB showed that NR cells are coupled into clusters that span the thickness of the retina and that rpe cells are both coupled together and to clusters of cells in the neural retina immunolabelling for cx showed this gap junction protein is present at the junction between the rpe and vz and thus could potentially mediate the coupling of the two tissues immunolabelling for betatubulin and vimentin showed that clusters of coupled cells in the NR comprised mainly progenitor cells we conclude that gap junctions between progenitor cells and between these cells and the rpe may orchestrate retinal proliferationdifferentiation via the propagation of ca or other signalling molecules

mucocutaneous candidiasis is frequently one of the first signs of hiv infection over of patients with aids will develop oropharyngeal candidiasis at some time during their illness although numerous antifungal agents are available azoles both topical clotrimazole and systemic fluconazole itraconazole have replaced older topical antifungals GV and nystatin in the management of OPC in these patients the systemic azoles itraconazole and fluconazole are generally safe and ERP agents in HIV+ patients with OPC a concern in these patients is clinical relapse which appears to be dependent on degree of immunosuppression and is more common following clotrimazole and ketoconazole than following fluconazole or itraconazole candida esophagitis is also of concern since it occurs in more than of patients with aids fluconazole is an integral part of the management of mucosal candidiasis a cyclodextrin oral solution formulation of itraconazole has clinical response rates similar to fluconazole and is an ERP alternative in patients with fluconazolerefractory mucosal candidiasis treatment options include itraconazole amphotericin b oral suspension and parenteral amphotericin b

fortyfour thyroid autonomously hyperfunctioning adenomas were analyzed to assess the frequency of mutations occurring in the tsh receptor tshr pcramplified fragments encompassing the entire exon of the tshr gene were obtained from the genomic dna extracted from the tumors and their adjacent normal tissues and were examined by direct nucleotide sequencing point mutations were found in of the adenomas examined one mutation occurred in codon asp to gly four in codon three were ala to ser one ala to val substitution two in codon both thr to ile and two in codon asp to tyr or his all the alterations were located in a part of the gene coding for an area including the i3 and the C6 TM domain of the tsh receptor all mutations were somatic and heterozygotic and none was simultaneous with alterations of ras or gsp oncogenes thus our data show that in our series of hyperfunctioning thyroid adenomas a somatic mutation of the tshr responsible for the constitutive activation of the camp pathway occurs in of the PT

short stretches of amino acids termed NLSs nls can mediate assembly of proteins into the nucleus proteins from the yeast saccharomyces cerevisiae have been identified that specifically recognize nuclear localization peptides silver p i sadler and m a osborne j cell biol we now further define the role of one of these nlsbinding proteins in nuclear protein localization the nlsbinding protein of kd molecular mass can be purified from salt extracts of nuclei antibodies raised against the nlsbinding protein localized the protein mainly to the nucleus with minor amounts in the cytoplasm these antibodies also inhibited the association of nlsprotein conjugates with nuclei incubation of nuclei with PRs coupled to agarose removed nlsbinding protein activity extracts EC for nlsbinding proteins can be added back to salt or proteasetreated nuclei to restore nlsbinding activity these results suggest that the first step of nuclear protein import can be reconstituted in vitro

the echoplanar imaging method epi and its SCV can acquire data sufficient to form an image in a much shorter time than the fourier or projectionreconstruction methods fundamental requirements on gradient waveforms and signal sampling are discussed for epi and sepi a variant using sinusoidal gradient waveforms based on the ktrajectory formulation of acquisition in nmr imaging using a new simulation method image defects in epi and sepi are demonstrated which result from failure to meet these requirements faithful rendition of theoretically ideal gradient waveforms places severe demands on gradient system response in rapid imaging it is shown however that nonideal gradient system response need not degrade images if appropriate reconstruction computations are used transfer functions and NPS are derived for epi and sepi and the ratio of these two functions is used to derive ideal observer signaltonoise ratios for simple visual interpretive tasks it is shown that ideal observer performance can differ significantly between epi and sepi for some visual tasks using the same nmr imaging system

a year old woman was admitted to the hospital with ASA shortly followed by respiratory failure and circulatory arrest despite intensive cardiopulmonary resuscitation during which extreme arterial hypoxaemia persisted the patient died postmortem examination revealed a PFO with a diameter of cm a severe righttoleft shunt initiated by acute pulmonary hypertension was considered to be the cause of death because of the relatively high prevalence of patent foramen ovale in the normal population we suggest this patency may enhance the risk of fatal outcome of ASA

nutrition screening is the process of identifying hospital patients with a high risk for nutrition problems who may require comprehensive nutrition assessment dietitians at a bed teaching hospital recently implemented a nutrition screening program remarkable for efficient use of existing personnel resources the threestep procedure includes a nutrition questionnaire completed by the patient in the hospital admissions office measurement of each patients height and weight by an admissions nurse and addition of the patients SS ALB plus summary and recommendations for nutrition MI by a registered dietitian the procedure reduces the time needed for individual DUE from to T2 and results in hour time saving per day per clinical dietitian patients designated as high risk by this method appear to be more seriously ill as shown by significantly longer hospitalization the nutrition screening procedure described is simple efficient and applicable to a wide variety of institutional settings

serious cardiac arrhythmias including IVT ventricular fibrillation TP and qt prolongation have been reported in patients taking cisapride with other drugs that inhibit the cytochrome pa hepatic enzyme this column will review the mechanism of action of cisapride the drugs metabolism and reported cardiac adverse effects with intravenous cisapride

hyperhomocysteinemia is as a risk factor for increased AVD in ldopatreated parkinson disease pd patients this effect of ldopa is associated with the metabolism of LD by methylation this study aimed to assess the effect of pergolide on HC levels HC C2 were compared between pd patients treated with pergolide as monotherapy with LD as monotherapy or with an ldopa and pergolide combination and compared with controls plasma homocysteine C2 were significantly higher in the ldopa monotherapy group and there were no significant differences for the pergolide treatment CG pergolide can be beneficial for increased plasma homocysteine levels

endotracheal suction ets is a common method used to remove bronchopulmonary secretions this study aimed to investigate whether and how nurses in the PIC environment assess the need for ET suction overt nonparticipant observation and interviews were used to collect data from nurses on four paediatric intensive care units the large majority of episodes of ets observed were performed in response to an identified clinical need based on changes in a variety of clinical signs and patient behaviour this study highlights the complex nature of nurses assessment of the need for ets and has implications for training and education of nursing staff

several findings have indicated that schizophrenia may be connected with the impaired epigenetic regulation of gene transcription the present study investigated the epigenetic modifications connected with histone h methylation at lysine k and k in the medial prefrontal SC mpfc in a neurodevelopmental model of schizophrenia based on prenatal administration of MAM mam at embryonic day which impairs the sensorimotor gating process in adult but not adolescent animals the effect of mam was determined at different PN ages prepuberty p p and p and postpuberty p and p using western blot analyses mam treatment altered the levels of hkme before puberty hkme was decreased at p and p but was increased at p in contrast hkme was noticeably decreased in AD rats immunofluorescence experiments revealed that hkme protein C2 were increased in neuronal cells at p and that hkme levels were decreased in astrocytes at p after mam administration decreases in the methyltransferase ashl protein C2 at p p and p were also observed while the protein levels of the methyltransferase ga did not NC in addition C2 of the demethylases lsd and jaridc were analysed after mam administration lsd protein levels were increased at p but decreased at p jaridc protein C2 were increased in the mamtreated animals at p decreased gad mrna C2 were found in adult mamtreated animals similar to alternation observed in schizophrenia the present study indicates that prenatal mam administration evokes changes in the methylation patterns of histone h during PN life

a large number of hospital applications are potentially interested in natural language processing since they currently heavily depend on an efficient use of a huge amount of textual information the need for systems that are able to accept multiple EU languages is of paramount interest as language barriers can be a strong impediment for largescale communication in europe in particular regarding telemedicine in the context of the aim project helios the NLP nlp component offers a large variety of medical services according to natural language free input it allows the multilingual analysis of medical texts currently in english french and german and the storage of the meaning of these texts under a deep knowledge representation that can be queried whenever it is needed in addition it provides facilities to handle knowledge source embedded into the conceptual typologies and into the dictionaries this article aims at describing all these functionalities and their integration into the environment of the helios project

temporal processing of binary mixtures results in odorants being perceived in series separated by many hundreds of ms since the odorant perceived first is the main suppressor knowledge of the order of perception of two odorants can allow prediction of interactions in mixtures the present T0 investigated the temporal coding of ternary mixtures composed of carvone coniferan and triethylamine and citralva lillial and TEA using a specially constructed airdilution olfactometer the results indicated that even though each of the components could be readily identified in ternary mixtures it was very difficult to indicate which odor was perceived first with chance scores being recorded the same outcome occurred even when the slowest odorant was delivered ms T3 the fastest to the nose it is proposed that olfactory memory gives precedence to identification of the components of mixtures rather than to their order of perception when more than two odorants are in a mixture

previous reports showed that breast and gastric cancers overexpressing cerbb protein have a greater metastatic potential and worse prognosis than tumors in which this protein is not overexpressed the present T0 was undertaken to examine the significance of cerbb protein expression as a prognostic factor in CC protein expression was examined immunohistologically in CC tissue from patients without distant metastasis from patients with CL metastasis and from patients with lung metastasis the cerbb proteinpositive rate was significantly higher in cases with lymphatic vessel invasion in the primary RT but it did not correlate with lymph node metastases expression of cerbb did not correlate with any other histologic feature histologic type pT venous vessel invasion or the clinical stage the positive rate in the primary lesion was significantly higher in cases with liver metastasis than in cases without liver metastasis the positive rate was significantly higher in the hepatic than in the primary lesions the expression of cerbb protein in CC tissue correlates closely with CL metastasis but not with lymphatic or lung metastases

chronic obstructive pulmonary disease copd is a CLD that is thought to affect over one million people in great britain the main factor contributing to the development of copd is TS this paper presents a microsimulation model for the development of copd incorporating population dynamics and trends in smoking the MM simulates a population longitudinally throughout their lifetimes providing projections of future copd prevalence and DUE of the effects of changes in risk factor prevalence such as smoking sensitivity analysis provides information on the most influential MM parameters the modelpredicted prevalence of copd in was in males over the age of years amongst nonsmokers and amongst sm and a modest decline over the next years due to recent trends in smoking rates the simulation MM provides us with valuable information on current and future trends in copd in great britain it was developed primarily to enable easy extension to evaluate the effects of occupational and environmental exposures on lung CF and the prevalence of copd and to allow DUE of interventions such as introducing health surveillance or policy changes as longitudinal studies for investigating copd are difficult due to the lengthy followup time required and the potentially large number of dropouts we anticipate that the model will provide a valuable tool for health impact assessment an D2 model for occupational exposures is under development and will be presented in a subsequent paper

to investigate the possible protective effects of nitric oxide no inhalation in newborns with meconium aspiration dold piglets were studied for h after an intratracheal bolus mlkg of a mgml mixture of human meconium twelve of the piglets were treated with continuous no inhalation at a dose of ppm n or ppm n started min before the insult pulmonary haemodynamics and SVR oxygenation were followed and lung tissue samples were studied for signs of inflammation evidence of ultrastructural injury and apoptotic cell changes inhalation of ppm no in contrast to ppm no significantly delayed the meconiuminduced pulmonary pressure rise and the increase in intrapulmonary shunt fraction and maintained better oxygenation in the piglets histologically and biochemically treatment with or ppm no inhalation did not protect the lungs against meconiuminduced inflammatory injury further ultrastructural lung tissue analysis revealed a significant amount of AM exudate and oedematous alveolar epithelium and endothelium T3 meconium instillation also in the lungs treated with no inhalation however the increase in AI epithelial cell deaths previously shown to be stimulated by intratracheal meconium was significantly impeded after inhalation of ppm these results thus show that early continuous no inhalation controls the rise in pulmonary CAP and improves the efficiency of arterial oxygenation and further prevents the increase in epithelial apoptosis but does not protect against early inflammatory damage caused by meconium aspiration

glycans can be olinked to proteins via the hydroxyl group of serine threonine IMT hydroxylysine or hydroxyproline sometimes the glycan is olinked to the hydroxyl group via a phosphodiester bond the core monosaccharide residue may be GalNAc nacetylglucosamine galactose glucose fucose mannose xylose or arabinose these olinked glycans can remain as a monosaccharide but often a complex structure is built up by stepwise addition of monosaccharides monosaccharides known to be added include galactose nacetylglucosamine fucose nacetylneuraminic acid nglycolylneuraminic acid and ketodeoxynonulosonic acid olinked glycans can also contain sulfate and phosphate residues this leads to the possibility of the existence of numerous oglycan structures the biological olinked DB bold is a relational DB that contains information on olinked glycan structures their biological sources with a link to the swissprot protein DB the references in which the glycan was described with a link to medline and the methods used to determine the glycan structure the DB provides a valuable resource for glycobiology researchers interested in olinked oligosaccharide structures that have been previously described on proteins from different species and tissues

illicit opiate addiction has emerged as a L1 problem in many western countries in the second half of this century and its social harm implications have become much exacerbated with the ON of the hiv epidemic in the s by now most western jurisdictions have resorted to MTD treatment as the most ERP and best researched intervention against the negative consequences of opiate addiction including mortality morbidity crime and loss of SF methadone treatment in canada features a long turbulent and instructive PH as an exemplary case study in public policy while both britain and the us experimented with opiate prescription treatment in the first half of the century canadian proposals for such programs initially never made it beyond the discussion stage largely due to the influential resistance from the law enforcement sector however in CS of growing influence from an emerging addictions treatment sector canada became the first western jurisdiction to experiment with methadone prescription for the treatment of opiate addiction forty years ago methadone treatment became quickly and widely established as an ERP treatment modality through the s but in the early s resistance from the law and health sectors evolved and triggered the government to establish a set of comprehensive and restrictive federal MTD treatment regulations which have dominated its realities until today almost completely regulated out of existence by the end of the s methadone treatments prevalence gradually increased again through the s and recent decentralization efforts to provincial levels earlier this decade have had dramatic effects on treatment availability significant events in the canadian PH of methadone treatment and its regulation reflect developments in the us and substantial recent domestic treatment expansion developmentsas well as renewed sparks of resistanceare reminiscent of MTD treatments patterns of PH as they developed in the late s this paper traces the turbulent PH of MTD treatment regulation and policy in canada with particular attention to institutional professional and political determinants at the complex intersection of health law and addiction

RT are one of themost common structural rearrangements with a frequency of and occur when there is an exchange of distal segments to breakpoints between nonhomologous chromosomes two or three independent simple reciprocal or Rb translocations coexist in the same carrier were classified as complex chromosome rearrangements ccrs structural chromosome rearrangements are considered balanced when there is no apparent gain or loss of chromosome material in majority of cases apparently balanced structural chromosome rearrangements abcr are not associated with abnormal phenotypes although these have been described in of de novo abcr and of apparently balanced ccr here we report a patient with de novo two apparently balanced RT and two partial monosomies one of these involving an independent chromosome characterized by microarray structural rearrangement investigations can improve the knowledge about human genome architecture and correlation of genomic imbalances to abnormal phenotype

the response of e coli to hg exposure was investigated using proteomic and metalloproteomic approaches e coli was cultured in the lb medium containing hgcl andor selenomethionine the growth curve of e coli was measured to estimate the toxicity of hg or selenomethionine T3 twodimensional gel electrophoresis de CSD of hg in de gel was detected with synchrotron radiation xray fluorescence srxrf at wb beijing synchrotron radiation facility the proteins with differential expression and those containing hg were identified with ESI tandem mass spectrometry esimsms and PMF analysis the results showed that hg can inhibit the growth of e coli while supplement of selenomethionine can shorten the lag period induced by hg indicating an antagonistic effect of selenomethionine against hg toxicity mechanistically hg was observed to be able to bind pyruvate kinase a glycolytic enzyme and modulate the expression of five other proteins including downregulation of outer membrane protein w and upregulation of transcription termination factor rho cysteine synthase TAL a and AhpC therefore our results indicated that mercury may influence osmosis of plasma membrane AOD and glycometabolism of the microorganism this study demonstrates the high sensitivity of srxrf in identifying metalassociated proteins compared to conventional proteomic approaches

we describe the anxiolyticlike effects of the first selective metabotropic gproteincoupled glutamate mglu IL-1ra nndibenzyhydrylethanediamine dihydrochloride amn as measured in the modified SIH sih and the fourplate tests administration of amn mgkg intraperitoneally to swiss mice produced anxiolyticlike effects in the modified sih and fourplate tests moreover it was ineffective as an anxiolytic in the sih test in mglu receptor knockout mice as compared with wildtype B6 littermate controls in contrast diazepam mgkg significantly reduced sih in both the wildtype and KO animals the anxiolyticlike effect of amn in the sih paradigm was abolished by i.c.v. with flumazenil mgkg intraperitoneally this indicates an involvement of GABA acidergic neurotransmission in amns anxiolytic actions the results indicate that activation of the mglu receptor produces anxiolyticlike effects via the modulation of the GABA acid system

HH is a common inherited disorder of iron metabolism in caucasian populations two mutations in the hfe gene are strongly associated with hereditary haemochromatosis one of these mutations cystyr cy is found homozygous in of subjects with typical hereditary HFE a second mutation hisasp hd has also been identified but is not associated with the same degree of iron overload as with the cy mutation about of subjects who are heterozygous for both mutations cy hdcompound heterozygotes can express typical hereditary haemochromatosis a C1 number of patients with early disease are asymptomatic and prompt diagnosis and treatment can result in normal life expectancy the diagnosis can readily be confirmed by SI studies and genetic testing for cy homozygotes or compound heterozygotes diagnosed under the age of years and with no biochemical or clinical evidence of liver disease phlebotomy therapy can be initiated without the need for CL biopsy liver biopsy should still be considered in all other patients with iron overload screening of first degree relatives should now be based on genotype assessment and measurement of SI parameters in order to determine phenotypic expression of the disease

EN is a skin disorder that is characterised by the presence of painful erythematous nodules and plaques mainly located on the shins this disorder is associated with comorbidities and can be considered as an aspecific reaction pattern to a broad range of causes however an idiopathic variant also exists which occurs in of cases EN is preeminently a skin disease which due to its multifactorial pathogenesis occurs in a wider field than dermatology CT therefore knowledge of this disorder is important for all practicing physicians to illustrate this we describe three patients with erythema nodosum attributable to different causes

lithium salts have been widely used for several years in the treatment of MDP various sideeffects of lithium salts have been described the present case report present two patients in whom SN dysfunction leading to syncope was caused by lithium one of the cases showed signs of depressed sinus node CF even when not on lithium but no PS arose until lithium treatment was commenced the second case showed no signs of depressed SN function when lithium was withdrawn to study the prevalence of SND in patients on lithium therapy consecutive patients on lithium were examined the examination included case PH ecg and carotid massage in two patients lithium could not be ruled out as being responsible for SN depression and in one patient the same was true for the atrioventricular node none of these patients had any PS it is concluded that lithium treatment may result in SN dysfunction this sideeffect is however not common lithium treatment can obviously be instituted in all patients without a history suggesting sinus node dysfunction patients with a history of dizziness andor syncope should not be given lithium until thorough cardiological examination has been carried out likewise a cardiological examination should be performed if patients on lithium develop PS of this type

the brains lifelong capacity for experiencedependent STDP allows adaptation to new environments or to changes in the environment and to changes in internal BB states such as occurs in BB damage since the initial discovery by hebb that EE ee was able to confer improvements in cognitive behavior ee has been investigated as a powerful form of experiencedependent STDP animal studies have shown that exposure to ee results in a number of molecular and morphological alterations which are thought to underpin changes in neuronal CF and ultimately behavior these consequences of ee make it ideally suited for investigation into its use as a potential therapy after neurological disorders such as traumatic BB injury tbi in this review we aim to first briefly discuss the effects of ee on SMB and neuronal CF followed by a review of the underlying molecular and structural changes that account for eedependent plasticity in the normal uninjured AD brain we then extend this review to specifically address the role of ee in the treatment of exp tbi where we will discuss the demonstrated sensorimotor and cognitive benefits associated with exposure to ee and their possible mechanisms finally we will explore the use of eebased rehabilitation in the treatment of human tbi patients highlighting the remaining questions regarding the effects of ee

rhodnius prolixus a bloodfeeding hemipteran insect ingests large meals which are followed by rapid diuresis to eliminate excess water and salt in rhodnius serotonin and an unidentified MMP have been shown to act as neurohormonal diuretic AF in other insects two families of diuretic MMP the ACTH releasing factor crflike and kinin peptides have been identified and sequenced recently we demonstrated the presence of a crflike diuretic peptide in the cns and digestive system of rhodnius using immunohistochemistry and bioassay in this study combining immunohistochemistry and radioimmunoassay ria techniques we show the presence of leucokininlike peptides in the cns and digestive system of rhodnius th instar additionally doublelabel immunohistochemistry demonstrates that the leucokininlike and crflike peptides are colocalized in the posterior lateral NS cells of the mesothoracic ganglionic mass mtgm and in neurohaemal areas on abdominal nerves one and two suggesting the possibility of corelease of the MMP into the hemolymphpartially purified extracts of the cns and neurohaemal tissue were tested in vitro on malpighian tubule secretion and camp assays the factors eluting with increasing acetonitrile percentages from seppak cartridges were assayed in the presence or absence of ketanserin a serotonin antagonist which blocks the effects of serotonin on malpighian tubules the results indicate activity of serotonin and a crflike diuretic peptide on rhodnius MT but fail to demonstrate activity of the leucokininlike MMP the rapid diuresis following feeding is a highly coordinated event requiring the movement of water and salt across the epithelial cells of the crop into the hemolymph and from the hemolymph across the cells of the malpighian tubules the urine then travels along the MT into the hindgut in order to be expelled the presence of a leucokininlike peptides in the cns and digestive system which colocalizes with a crflike MMP suggests that kinins may play a role in the rapid diuresis although possibly not directly on the malpighian tubules

tubulinopathies are increasingly emerging L1 causes underlying complex cerebral malformations particularly in case of microlissencephaly often associated with hypoplastic or absent CS tracts FADS fads refers to a clinically and genetically heterogeneous group of disorders with congenital malformations related to impaired fetal movement we report on an early foetal case with fads and microlissencephaly due to tubbb mutation neuropathological examination disclosed virtually absent cortical lamination foci of neuronal overmigration into the leptomeningeal spaces CC agenesis cerebellar and BS hypoplasia and extremely severe hypoplasia of the spinal cord with no anterior and posterior horns and almost no MNs at the cellular level the pcysphe tubbb mutant leads to tubulin heterodimerization impairment decreased ability to incorporate into the CSK microtubule dynamics alteration with an accelerated rate of depolymerization to our knowledge this is the first case of microlissencephaly to be reported presenting with a so severe and early form of fads highlighting the importance of tubulin mutation screening in the context of fads with microlissencephaly

in order to evaluate its usefulness in the assessment of RP gallium citrate ga imaging was performed before and after radiation therapy rt on patients with LC in patients with RP detected radiographically abnormal ga uptake in sites other than tumors was found in all postrt ga lung images three patterns of uptake were found a focal uptake corresponding to the rt field n b diffuse uptake including the rt field n and c diffuse uptake outside the rt field n the area of ga uptake was consistent with that of interstitial pneumonitis as revealed histopathologically in cases ga uptake in pattern c was an indicator of poor prognosis for the patients with radiation pneumonitis ga uptake in the patients with reversible pneumonitis disappeared with steroid therapy sixteen of asymptomatic patients in whose CXRs there was no finding of RP showed transient ga uptake these were considered to occur in the subclinical RP these data suggest that ga imaging is more sensitive than CXR in the detection of RP and is useful in the assessment of the extent and clinical course of RP

the temporal pattern of cone outersegment disc shedding was examined in the retina of the california ground squirrel spermophilus beecheyi under two different lighting conditions squirrels were entrained to either lx room lighting or lx cone shedding during the dark period was biphasic in both conditions with increases occurring at hr and hr after light offset entrainment to lx resulted in an increase in shedding at hr after CS offset and a slight advance in the timing of both peaks dense LG were often found in photoreceptors retinal neurons mÃ¼ller cells microglia and vascular cells these granules which were found primarily during the dark period and early light period were lipofuscinlike in their lipophilia size and autofluorescence many of the LG were probably autophagic in origin but some within mÃ¼ller cells may have originated via endocytosis of ECM which was exocytosed by photoreceptors

hibernation is an adaptive strategy used by some animals to cope with cold and food shortage the heart rate overall SE need body temperature and many other physiological functions are greatly reduced during torpor but promptly return to normal C2 upon arousal the heartbeat of torpid bats can be hundreds fold lower than that of AS bats indicating that hibernating bats have a remarkable ability to control ECC in cardiac muscle fkbpb calstabin a peptidylprolyl cistrans isomerase is critical for the regulation of ECC whether fkbpb is adapted to hibernation in bats is not known evolutionary analyses showed that the Ï values of the fkbpb genes of mammalian species are all less than and amino acid sequence alignments revealed that fkbpb proteins are highly conserved in mammals the expression of the fkbpb gene was found to be elevated at both mrna and protein levels in two distantly related bats rhinolophus ferrumequinum in yinpterochiroptera and myotis ricketti in yangochiroptera during torpor transcription factors such as yy and sps were bioinformatically determined to have a higher IB affinity to the potential regulatory regions of fkbpb genes in hibernating than in nonhibernating mammals this study provides new insights into the molecular evolution of fkbpb in adaptation to bat hibernation

chiari type i malformation is a congenital disorder that is characterized by the caudal extension of the cerebellar tonsils through the foramen magnum into the C2 canal and by a reduced PF voen exposed to the same ecological gradients the stickleback family is a good model with which to explore the ecological and genetic basis of parallel and nonparallel patterns of phenotypic evolution because there are a variety of populations and species that are locally adapted to divergent environments although the patterns of phenotypic divergence as well as the genetic and ecological mechanisms have been well characterized in threespine sticklebacks gasterosteus aculeatus we know little about the patterns of phenotypic diversification in other stickleback lineages in eastern hokkaido japan there are three species of ninespine sticklebacks pungitius tymensis and the FW type and the brackishwater type of the p pungitiusp sinensis species complex they utilize divergent habitats along coaststream gradients of rivers here we investigated genetic ecological and phenotypic divergence among three species of japanese ninespine sticklebacks divergence in trophic morphology and salinity tolerance occurred in the direction predicted by the patterns observed in threespine sticklebacks however the patterns of divergence in armour plate were different from those previously found in threespine sticklebacks furthermore the genetic basis of SP R2 may differ from that in threespine sticklebacks because threespine sticklebacks are wellestablished MM for evolutionary research the sympatric trio of ninespine sticklebacks will be an invaluable resource for ecological and genetic studies on both common and lineagespecific patterns of phenotypic diversification

ATP access to the activesite cavity of SHC from alicyclobacillus acidocaldarious and lanosterol synthase osc OSC from saccharomyces cerevisiae was studied by an inhibition mutagenesis and homologymodelling approach crystal structure and homology modelling indicate that both enzymes possess a narrow constriction that separates an entrance lipophilic channel from the activesite cavity the role of the constriction as a mobile gate that permits ATP passage was investigated by experiments in which critically located cys residues either present in native protein or inserted by SDM were labelled with specifically designed thiolreacting molecules some amino acid residues of the yeast enzyme selected on the basis of sequence alignment and a homology MM were individually replaced by residues bearing side chains of different lengths charges or hydrophobicities in some of these mutants substitution severely reduced enzymatic activity and thermal stability homology modelling revealed that in these mutants some critical stabilizing interactions could no longer occur the possible critical role of entrance channel and constriction in specific substrate recognition by eukaryotic osc is discussed

SVR inflammation is strongly involved in the pathophysiology of the metabolic syndrome a cluster of metabolic risk AF that includes HTG aspirin treatment lowers inflammation via inhibition of nfÎºb activity but also reduces HTG in humans the aim of this study was to investigate the mechanism by which aspirin improves hypertriglyceridemia human apolipoprotein ci apociexpressing mice apoc mice an rat with elevated plasma triglyceride tg C2 as well as normolipidemic wildtype wt mice were fed a highfat diet hfd and treated with aspirin aspirin treatment reduced hepatic nfÎºb activity in hfdfed apoc and wt mice and in addition aspirin decreased plasma tg levels p in HTG apoc mice this tglowering effect could not be explained by enhanced vldltg clearance but aspirin selectively reduced hepatic production of vldltg in both apoc p and wt mice p without affecting vldlapob production aspirin did not alter hepatic expression of genes involved in fa oxidation lipogenesis and vldl production but decreased the incorporation of plasmaderived fa by the liver into vldltg p which was independent of hepatic expression of genes involved in fa uptake and transport we conclude that aspirin improves hypertriglyceridemia by decreasing vldltg production without affecting vldl particle production therefore the inhibition of inflammatory pathways by aspirin could be an interesting target for the treatment of HTG

a method in time domain is proposed to investigate resonance raman spectra of absorbed molecules on semiconductor surfaces the charge transfer at the moleculesurface interface is incorporated with the use of an andersonnewns type hamiltonian where the surface continuum state is dealt with an expansion of legendre polynomials for fast numerical convergence from a model test it is found that the intensities of raman modes in the sole molecule generally decrease as the moleculesurface interaction is switched on except that the energy gaps between the molecular excited state and the bottom of the band are at special values new raman peaks which are not observed in the sole molecule however appear and are greatly enhanced the enhancement depends on the electronic coupling and the energy gap it is also highly CS to the modespecific lambda in the charge transfer state and a thousand times enhancement can be obtained at a certain lambda the corresponding electron dynamics is revealed by the population decay from the absorbed molecule

an accurate diagnosis of burns and PUs in the early stages can be made by computerized image processing this study describes a critical assessment of potential methodologies for noninvasive wound evaluation using a color imaging system we also developed a method for quantifying histological readings and applied these techniques to a porcine rat of wound formation differences in calibrated hue between injured and noninjured skin provided a repeatable differentiation of wound severity for situations when the time of injury was known this color analysis allowed statistically significant differentiation of mild moderate and severe injuries within minutes after the application of the injury it was more difficult to distinguish wound severity one to four days later however the correlation reemerged when the wounds were five to seven days old this technique could be adapted for assessing and tracking wound severity in humans in a clinical setting

although nitric oxide no has received extensive attention as an antihypertrophic agent the mechanisms underlying its regulation of endothelin et have not been fully elucidated since rhoa has been identified as an important mediator of cardiac hypertrophy and is inhibited by no in vascular tissue we sought to determine whether the antiet effects of no in cardiomyocytes were mediated via inhibition of the rhoarock cascade in the context of cardiac hypertrophy neonatal rat ventricular myocytes were cultured in the presence of et nm with or without pretreatment with the no SNAP snap microm brcgmp cgmp microm the rhoa inhibitor c exoenzyme c ngml or the rock inhibitor y microm etinduced cardiomyocyte hypertrophy was prevented by pretreatment with snap cgmp c or y the hypertrophic response to et was associated with significantly increased gene and protein expression of both nos and nos although nos was unaffected et treatment for min increased MB rhoa fold p which was prevented by both snap and cgmp p these effects were associated with a CR abrogation of etinduced phosphorylation of the downstream target of rhoa cofilin that was mimicked by direct inhibition of rhoa and rock in addition confocal microscopy and western blotting revealed that h et treatment reduced the g to factin ratio p which was prevented by snap cgmp c and y p taken together these results suggest that the antihypertrophic effects of no are due in part to cgmpdependent inhibition of the rhoarockcofilin signalling pathway these findings may be important in understanding the mechanisms of antiet and antihypertrophic effects of no as well as in the development of novel rhoatargeted therapeutic interventions for treating CH

the dominant endogenous clock named the SCN nucleus scn regulates circadian rhythms of behavioral and physiological activity in mammals one of the main characteristics of the scn is that the animal maintains a circadian rhythm with a period close to h in the absence of a daily lightdark cycle called the free running period the free running period varies among species due to heterogeneity of the scn network previous studies have shown that the heterogeneity in cellular coupling as well as in intrinsic neuronal periods shortens the free running period furthermore as derived from experiments one SN coupling strength is negatively associated with its period it is unknown what the effects of this association between coupling strength and period are on the free running period and how the heterogeneity in coupling strength influences this free running period in the present study we found that in the presence of a negative relationship between one neurons coupling strength and its period surprisingly the dispersion of coupling strengths increases the free running period our present finding may shed new light on the understanding of the heterogeneous scn network and provides an alternative explanation for the diversity of free running periods between species

the purposes of this T0 were to determine and compare the single and multipledose pharmacokinetics of cefepime in patients with and without cystic fibrosis twelve patients with cystic fibrosis hospitalized for treatment of acute pulmonary exacerbations were studied in addition pharmacokinetic data for seven of the patients with cystic fibrosis were compared with those for seven agematched CP the cefepime dose was mgkg of body weight maximum g po as a min intravenous infusion every h for a minimum of days serial plasma and urine samples obtained after the first and last doses were analyzed for cefepime content by a validated highpressure liquid chromatographic CA by standard noncompartmental analysis the pharmacokinetic parameters ascertained were AUC in plasmatime curve beta halflife total body clearance renal clearance and volume of distribution at Css in addition the maximum concentration in plasma was recorded mean standard deviation results of the first dose analysis in patients with cystic fibrosis were as follows Cmax in plasma microgramsml area under the concentration in plasmatime curve microgramshml beta halflife h TB clearance mlmin renal clearance mlminkg volume of distribution at steady state liters analysis for the last dose in patients with cystic fibrosis did not vary appreciably from these values nor did those from the controls thus it appears that the firstdose pharmacokinetics of cefepime are predictive of those at steady state in order to consistently exceed the mic for pseudomonas aeruginosa for the entire tau in patients with cystic fibrosis a higher dose andor different tau compared with those used in this study may be necessary

assistive technologies at are tools that enhance the independence safety and quality of life of older people with PET limitations while at may extend independence in ageing there are racial and ethnic disparities in latelife at use with lower rates reported among hispanic older populations the aim of this study was to identify barriers experienced by hispanic communityliving older adults for using at sixty hispanic older adults years and older with PET limitations participated in this study a descriptive qualitative research design was used guided by the principles of the human activity AT MM to gain indepth understanding of participants perspectives regarding barriers to using at devices individual indepth semistructure interviews were conducted using the AT devices cards atdc assessment as a prompt to facilitate participants qualitative responses data analysis included descriptive statistics and rigorous thematic content analysis lack of at awareness and information cost of at limited coverage of at by heath care plans and perceived complexity of at were the predominant barriers experienced by the participants a multilevel RPA is required for a better understanding of the barriers for using at devices the personal contextual and activitybased barriers found in this T0 can be used to develop culturally CS at interventions to reduce existent disparities in independent living disabilities among older hispanics

transmission of sensory information through the dorsal thalamus involves two types of modalityrelated nuclei first order and higher order between which there are thought to be no intrathalamic interactions we now show that within the somatosensory thalamus cells in one nucleus the VB can influence activity in another nucleus the medial division of the po stimulation of VB cells evoked GABA postsynaptic currents in cells of the medial division of the posterior complex these currents exhibited the reversal potential and pharmaystemic osmoregulation in vivo the secretion of this hormone in the NH is primarily determined by the rate of action potential discharge of the magnocellular NSC mncs in the hypothalamus experiments done over the past years have clarified much of the neurophysiological basis underlying this important osmoregulatory reflex as discussed here recent findings indicate that the regulation of the FR of mncs during changes in SVR osmolality involves the concerted modulation of MS ion channels in mncs as well as excitatory glutamatergic inputs derived from forebrain regions such as the organum vasculosum of the LT

in the present work we demonstrate a novel RPA to improve the sensitivity of the out of lab portable CE measurements nowadays many signal enhancement methods are i underused nonoptimal ii overused distorts the data or iii inapplicable in fieldportable instrumentation because of a lack of computational power the described innovative migration velocityadaptive moving average method uses an optimal averaging window size and can be easily implemented with a microcontroller the CCD was used as a model for the development of a signal processing method and the demonstration of its impact on the sensitivity the frequency characteristics of the recorded electropherograms and peaks were clarified higher electrophoretic mobility analytes exhibit higherfrequency peaks whereas lower electrophoretic mobility analytes exhibit lowerfrequency peaks on the basis of the obtained data a migration velocityadaptive moving average algorithm was created adapted and programmed into capillary PACE dataprocessing software employing the developed algorithm each data point is processed depending on a certain migration time of the analyte because of the implemented migration velocityadaptive moving average method the SNR ratio improved up to times for sampling frequency of hz and up to times for sampling frequency of hz this paper could potentially be used as a methodological guideline for the development of new smoothing algorithms that require adaptive conditions in capillary PACE and other separation methods

drugs for CA and hiv infection tend to be admitted to the market on the basis of results from phase trials assessing the benefitrisk balance with phase trials often is difficultthe effect of the drug is usually temporary the rho between response or improvement of clinical measurements and the patients wellbeing is often poor and the side effects of drugs for these fatal diseases are serious therefore although sometimes difficult to conduct comparative trials that use ST placebos or BSC remain the cornerstone for reliably assessing the benefitrisk balance

cardiac myocytes synthesize and secrete a family of peptide hormones with potent natriuretic diuretic and vasodilatory properties these MMP are derived from precursor molecules that are encoded by two different genes the atrial natriuretic peptide F0 a nppa and the BNP or natriuretic peptide precursor b nppb a human genomic clone for the nppb gene was used to determine the chromosomal location of the nppb gene analysis of SB hybridization to dnas from various somatic cell hybrids and fluorescence in situ hybridization allowed assignment of the nppb locus to HSA p this location coincided with that of the nppa locus pulsedfield gel electrophoresis placed nppa and nppb within kb of each other this close chromosomal linkage together with the conserved primary sequences and structural organization of the two natriuretic peptide precursor genes suggests that the natriuretic peptide loci may have evolved from a common ancestor gene

the abilities of three synthetic OS to accelerate AT inhibition of ten clotting or fibrinolytic proteinases were compared with those of unfractionated fractionated highaffinity and lowmolecularweight heparins the results show that the AC effects of the latter three heparins under conditions approximating physiologic are exerted almost exclusively by acceleration of the inactivation of thrombin factor xa and factor ixa to near diffusioncontrolled rate constants of approximately ms all other proteinases are inhibited with at least fold lower rate constants the AC ability of the synthetic regular fondaparinux and highaffinity idraparinux pentasaccharides is due to a common mechanism involving acceleration of only factor xa inhibition to rate constants of approximately ms a synthetic hexadecasaccharide containing both the pentasaccharide CS and a proteinase IB site exerts its anticoagulant effect by accelerating AT inactivation of both thrombin and factor xa to rate constants of approximately ms although thrombin appears to be the more important target in contrast factor ixa inhibition is appreciably less stimulated the conformational NC of AT induced both by the pentasaccharides and longer heparins contributes substantially approximately fold to accelerating the inactivation of AF xa ixa and viia and moderately approximately fold to that of factor xiia and tissue plasminogen activator inhibition the bridging effect due to binding of antithrombin and proteinase to the same long heparin chain is dominating approximately fold for thrombin inhibition and is appreciably smaller although up to approximately fold for the inactivation of factors ixa and xia these results establish the proteinase targets of heparin derivatives currently used in or considered for thrombosis therapy and give new insights into the mechanism of heparin acceleration of AT inhibition of proteinases

GPI gpi anchors of all species contain the core structure proteinconhchpomanalphamanalphamanalphaglcnalphainositolpolipid in recent studies in yeast it was found that gpi mutants accumulate m an abnormal intermediate having the structure manalphanhchpomanalphaglcnalphaacylinositolpolipid it thus was realized that yeast gpi lipids as their mammalian counterparts contain an additional phosphorylethanolamine side chain on the alphalinked mannose the biosynthetic origin of this PE group was investigated using gpi deltaept deltacpt a strain which is unable to synthesize phosphatidylethanolamine by transferring phosphorylethanolamine from cdpethanolamine onto diacylglycerol but which still can make phosphatidylethanolamine by decarboxylation of phosphatidylserine gpi deltaept deltacpt triple mutants are unable to incorporate hethanolamine into m although metabolic labeling with hinositol demonstrates that they make as much m as gpi in contrast when labeled with hserine the triple mutant incorporates more label into m than gpi this result establishes that the PE group on the alphalinked mannose is derived from phosphatidylethanolamine and not from cdpethanolamine

this T0 examined whether the inflammatory mediators interleukin ilbeta and betaglucuronidase betag are present in the GCF gcf of children undergoing RPE and whether their C2 vary upon activation of the appliance and movement of the maxillary first molars nine adolescent patients who needed palatal expansion were studied each patient received a periodontal prophylaxis and instruction in proper home care including rinsing with chlorhexidine four weeks later a modified hyrax appliance was inserted the jackscrew was G1 twice daily until the appropriate expansion was achieved gcf samples were collected at i.c.v. observation periods and observation periods after placement of the appliance samples were collected with filter paper strips and analyzed by means of elisa and timedependent fluorometry for ilbeta and betag respectively the values recorded at the observation period weeks after the periodontal prophylaxis were used as baseline paired t tests were used to compare mediator levels at this baseline to the levels obtained at each of the subsequent observations the results indicate that betag and ilbeta are present in gcf of young HI their levels decrease following a strict regimen of plaque control orthodonticorthopedic forces evoke changes in the levels of the inflammatory mediators ilbeta and betag in the periodontal tissues that can be detected in gcf the results of this T0 support the hypothesis that mechanical CS- causes an inflammatory reaction within the periodontal tissues which in turn may trigger the biological processes associated with bone remodeling

the fmr gene contains an unstable cgg repeat in its untranslated region premutation alleles range between and repeat units and confer a risk for developing fragile xassociated tremorataxia syndrome or fragile xassociated primary ovarian insufficiency furthermore the premutation allele often expands to a FM during female germline transmission giving rise to the fragile x syndrome the risk for a premutation to expand depends mainly on the number of cgg units and the presence of agg interruptions in the cgg repeat unfortunately the detection of agg interruptions is hampered by technical difficulties here we demonstrate that singlemolecule sequencing enables the determination of not only the repeat size but also the complete repeat sequence including agg interruptions in male and female alleles with repeats ranging from to cgg units we envision this method will facilitate research and diagnostic analysis of the fmr repeat expansion

embryo implantation necessitates that the APM of uterine epithelial cells acquires adhesiveness recent studies have indicated that modulation of a L1 element of the epithelial phenotype ie apicalbasal cell polarity might be critical in this respect here we analyze polar characteristics of nonadhesive vs adhesive uterine epithelial cell lines focusing on cytoskeletaljunctional interactions that may play a role in regulating adhesiveness of the apical BPM heca is a human uterine epithelial cell line exhibiting nonadhesive properties of its apical surface for trophoblast whereas rl represent another such cell L1 exhibiting adhesive properties enabling trophoblast attachment homotypic intercellular contacts and functionally related proteins ie zo EC alphacatenin betacatenin PG and DP were examined by transmission electron microscopy immunocytochemistry confocal LSM and immunoprecipitation techniques in addition details of actin filament architecture were studied after phalloidin labeling while nonadhesive heca exhibited the wellknown DP of celltocell contacts of polarized epithelial cells adhesive rl showed a lack of zo expression tracer leakiness of the paracellular pathway and atypical features in adherens junctions ecadherin alphacatenin and PG were colocalized in all plasma membrane domains and betacatenin was localized in lateral membrane domains immunoprecipitations showed in both cell lines the presence of two different ecadherincatenin complexes one composed of EC alphacatenin and betacatenin and the other of ecadherin alphacatenin and PG concerning rl these data indicate that ecadherinplakoglobin complexes are randomly distributed whereas ecadherinbetacatenin complexes are laterally localized in these cells additionally the actinbased cytoskeleton of rl lacked a polar organization with respect to the intermediate filamentdesmosome system both cell types expressed DP i but the vast majority of rl lacked wellformed desmosomes as demonstrated by electron microscopy it is concluded that modulation of tight junctions andor remodelling of adherens junctions eg differential CSD of ecadherinplakoglobin complexes and ecadherinbetacatenin complexes are correlated with the development of apical adhesiveness of human uterine epithelial cells this MM system should allow to test experimentally whether this correlation is due to any causal function in the development of epithelial cell polarity

frkrak belongs to a novel family of src kinases with epithelial tissuespecific expression although developmental expression patterns and PET overexpression in vitro have associated these kinases with growth suppression and differentiation their physiological functions remain largely unknown we therefore generated mice carrying a null mutation in iyk the mouse homolog of frkrak we report here that frkrak mice are viable show similar growth rates to wildtype animals and are fertile furthermore a year study of health and survival did not identify differences in the incidence and spectrum of spontaneous tumors or provide evidence of hyperplasias in frkrak epithelial tissues histological analysis of organs failed to reveal any morphological changes in epithelial tissues that normally express high levels of frkrak ultrastructural analysis of intestinal enterocytes did not identify defects in brush border morphology or structural polarization demonstrating that frkrak is dispensable for intestinal cytodifferentiation additionally frkraknull mice do not display altered sensitivity to intestinal damage induced by ionizing radiation cdna microarray analysis revealed an increase in csrc expression and identified subtle changes in the expression of genes regulated by thyroid hormones significant decreases in the circulating C2 of t but not t hormone are consistent with this observation and reminiscent of ESS a stressassociated clinical condition

ST feeding up to days of an atherogenic diet to B6 LD lipoprotein receptordeficient mice did not result in decreased hepatic paraoxonase pon mrna but caused a dramatic decrease in plasma pon activity and mass the decreased activity and mass were temporally related to an increase in plasma and HD lipoprotein hdl lipid hydroperoxides and to a decrease in hdl cholesterol and native apolipoprotein ai apoai and apolipoprotein aii apoaii as the native apoai protein disappeared from the circulation higher molecular weight forms of apoai appeared some of which contained epitopes recognized by an antibody eo that recognizes Ox phospholipids T3 mice consumed an atherogenic diet for or days switching the mice to a LF chow diet for days resulted in a return to baseline C2 of lipid hydroperoxides but only a small return toward baseline for hdl cholesterol with no significant increase in apoai mass or pon activity and mass T3 mice consumed an atherogenic diet for days switching to the chow diet for days did not significantly alter the high molecular weight forms of apoai or the signal generated by eo in marked contrast after mice consumed an AD for days switching to the chow diet for days resulted in a dramatic increase in native apoai to baseline C2 with virtual disappearance of the high molecular weight forms of apoai including the form recognized by eo T3 mice consumed an AD for days switching to the chow diet for days also resulted in significant increases in hdl cholesterol and pon mass and activity although baseline C2 were not reached igg and igm antibodies were found to be associated with apoai in control animals were minimally decreased after the day AD were dramatically decreased after the day atherogenic diet and returned to near or above baseline levels after a return to the chow diet for days we conclude that the AD rapidly induces lipid hydroperoxide formation and apoai oxidation with the formation of high molecular weight forms of apoai concomitant with these changes in apoai C2 hdl cholesterol and pon activity and mass declined without changes in mrna levels for apoai or pon suggesting increased clearance of these altered hdl particles we further conclude that between the third and seventh day of the atherogenic diet an asyetunidentified mechanism for clearing the high molecular weight forms of apoai is induced and that this mechanism may be related to the clearance of immune complexes

human protein phosphatase nuclear targeting subunit pnuts plays critical roles in dna repair cell growth and survival the NTD of pnuts mediates interactions with tox and the PTEN pten which are essential for the roles of this protein to study this nterminal domain we have established its recombinant overproduction in e coli utilizing nusa F0 upon removal of the tag the remaining pnuts sample is soluble and highly pure we have characterized the domain using circular dichroism and nuclear magnetic resonance and analyzed its CS using bioinformatics all data agree in suggesting that the pnuts nterminal segment adopts a compact globular fold rich in Î±helical content where the folded fraction is substantially larger than the previously annotated fold we conclude that this domain adopts a single fold likely being an D2 form of the transcription factor sii leucinetryptophan conservedmotif thermal denaturation yielded a melting temperature of Â°c confirming the stability of the fold these findings pave the way for the molecular characterization of PET interactions mediated by the nterminal region of pnuts

three ultrastructural cytochemical methods have been used to classify the innervation of the rat renal SC every axon seen contained chromaffinreactive DCVs and stained for IMT hydroxylase indicating that they were all catecholaminergic about of the axons associated with smooth muscle and juxtaglomerular cells of the arteriolar vessels also contained DDC but this enzyme was not present in any of the peritubular axons our results are compatible with the possibility that in the rat the juxtaglomerular blood vessels but not the renal tubules are supplied by DA as well as by noradrenergic SN

a moderately differentiated human endometrial adenocarcinoma heterotransplanted into nude mice was investigated for morphological and molecular changes in the tumours after treating the animals with estradiol the TG was previously characterised as estradiolindependent but responsive inhibited without any changes in cell proliferation in response to hormonal treatment rather the cell loss factor increased in this experiment tumours influenced by estradiol were investigated at different timepoints T3 treatment by an in situ labelling technique to detect cells undergoing dna fragmentation as a sign of apoptosis expression of the apoptosis related protein bcl was evaluated by WB tumours from animals treated with estradiol showed an increase in tumour VDT from days to days compared to control tumours histologically tumours influenced by estradiol were better differentiated than control tumours and showed a significant increase in cells IF positively with the in situ apoptosis PCD technique a parallel time dependent decreased expression of bcl protein was observed these results confirm our previous findings where estradiol influenced the cell loss factor without changes in the growth fraction indicating increased AI activity in response to hormonal treatment

the performance of the major escherichia coli coldshock promoter in directing the synthesis of recombinant proteins at low temperatures was investigated in batch fermentations using a plasmidencoded transcriptional gene fusion between the cspa promoter region and the lacz gene rapid synthesis of betagalactosidase was observed when the fermentation BB was chilled to degreesc using a variety of cooling profiles including one modeling the heattransfer characteristics of a l pilot plant unit a linear cooling rate of degreescmin led to optimum REC yields for all singletemperature downshift experiments however the promoter became repressed min after initiation of cooling both temperature cycling between and degreesc and stepwise temperature downshifts between and degreesc led to multiple inductions of the cspa promoter nevertheless highefficiency reinduction was only observed during the first temperature pulse when the former strategy was used and when the cells were held at intermediate temperatures for less than min or more than min in the case of successive downshifts promoter repression was abolished in host cells bearing a null mutation in the gene encoding the ribosomal PBF rbfa leading to the constitutive and highlevel expression of betagalactosidase for h postshift when shake flask cultures were transferred from to degreesc the suitability of rbfa cells for cspadriven recombinant protein production was confirmed in highdensity fedbatch fermentations our results are consistent with the existence of a coldshockinduced repressor molecule that must accumulate at a threshold concentration before interfering with the production of proteins placed under cspa transcriptional control

electrophoretic surveys have demonstrated that populations of the sea anemone metridium senile along the northeast coast of the united states are polymorphic at four enzyme loci PGI pgi has two alleles in most populations phosphoglucomutase pgm has three alleles and two leucine aminopeptidase loci have two common alleles each PGI displays clinial R2 and an apparent association with environmental temperature phosphoglucomutase shows clinial R2 north of cape cod for two of the three alleles while the two leucine aminopeptidase loci are not clinial all loci show a great deal of R2 in populations on cape cod but there is no apparent systematic DP of this R2 temperature may be a selective agent in the maintenance of the pgi and pgm clines although other possibilities cannot presently be completely excluded

VBI is a wellknown syndrome but no corresponding hemodynamic deficit has yet been established we propose to define nuclear hemodynamic VBI on the basis of an oligemia lower than ml g per minute in the brain stemcerebellar region with use of the xenon xe inhalation method fifteen patients fulfilling this criterion underwent fourvessel angiography computed tomography and a standardized neurologic examination an acetazolamide test showed poor CR in more than half of the patients sometimes specifically in the vertebrobasilar area with use of singlephoton emission computed tomography and intravenous technetium tc mlabeled HMPAO in two cases the considerable decrease of regional cerebral blood flow in the BB stemcerebellar region was confirmed an excellent correlation was observed between the existence of nuclear hemodynamic vertebrobasilar insufficiency and angiographically proved arterial occlusions the dominant nuclear oligemic zone was regularly on the side of the anatomic arterial chief lesion clinical manifestations included rare transient ischemic attacks in one of patients intermittent BA symptoms in of patients and a subacute vertebrobasilar threatening syndrome thus imaging of a nuclear hemodynamic VB deficit provides an objective basis to the diagnosis of VBI and useful objective data for revascularization surgery

the erbb family of receptor TKs mediates activation of a wide network of signaling pathways erbb has weak kinase activity but its six docking sites for the p S1 of phosphoinositide kinase make it an important contributor to proliferative signaling erbb has a relatively short halflife but the exact mechanisms controlling its turnover are unclear as contradictory reports exist erbbmediated signaling is however negatively regulated by endocytosis of the receptors followed by either recycling or degradation our previous studies showed that erbb can be endocytosed and degraded in the absence of its ligand HRG however binding of heregulin increased the Kd rate in the current T0 we have investigated in more detail the trafficking and degradation of erbb in the presence or absence of HRG we report that erbb is internalized by clathrinmediated endocytosis both in the presence and absence of HRG moreover we show that both proteasomal and lysosomal activity regulate erbb Kd although steadystate expression of erbb is regulated by proteasomal activity to a C1 extent probably linked to a previously identified erlocalized quantity control the results indicate that internalization both constitutive and ligandinduced causes lysosomal Kd of erbb furthermore we show that erbb interacts with the escrt subunit hrs both in the presence and absence of HRG this indicates an escrtmediated sorting of erbb to late endosomes and lysosomes and in L1 with this we show that impaired escrt function leads to an endosomal accumulation of erbb
