ischemiareperfusion ir injury is a commonly encountered clinical problem and occurs probably as a consequence of irreversible mitochondrial injury the increased susceptibility of fatty livers to ICM injury is associated with depletion of adenosine triphosphate atp content which is preserved by preconditioning mitochondria being the main atp production source for the cell we aimed to evaluate whether ICM preconditioning ipc of fatty livers prevents the impairment in mitochondrial CF induced by ir lean and steatotic animals were subjected to min of hepatic warm ischemia and h of reperfusion ipc effect was tested in fatty livers after reperfusion serum alanine aminotransferase alt and aspartate aminotransferase ast C2 were measured mitochondrial membrane potential mitochondrial QO2 and susceptibility to mitochondrial permeability transition mpt were evaluated as well as atpase activity and adenine nucleotides ipc of fatty livers decreased SS ast and alt C2 fatty animals subjected to ir exhibited decreased mitochondrial membrane potential and a delay in the repolarization T3 a phosphorylation cycle associated with increased state QO2 increased tolerance to mpt induction preservation of ffoatpsynthase activity and mitochondrial bioenergetics were observed in ischemic PC fatty livers thus ipc is an endogenous protecting mechanism that preserves mitochondrial function and bioenergetics in fatty livers

there is considerable evidence that infection by avian lymphoid leukosis viruses can led to RT development in the target organ of the host the mechanism by which virusinduced oncogenic transformation occurs however is not clearly understood as a first step toward deciphering this process we have characterized the proviruses of the LL viruses in dnas extracted from the leukotic and metastatic tumors by using restriction enzyme digestion and filter hybridization analysis with radioactive probes specific for the infecting genome our results indicate i that LL tumors are clonal in origin ii that there are multiple CS in the cellular genome of the target tissue where the virus dna can integrate and that in the majority of the PT at least one such site of each tumor is adjacent to a cellular sequence related to the oncogene of mc virus and iii that deletions and other structural alterations in the proviral dna may facilitate tumorigenesis

successful intercarpal arthrodesis requires a SD fusion with maintenance of correct alignment and spatial relationship of the carpus the technique described utilizes a series of tube saws to fashion the arthrodesis bed and then insert a sized IC dowel bone graft with a tight interference fit this technique has been used in patients over a twoyear period in both medial and lateral column intercarpal fusions all wrists had fused by the tenth postoperative month the technique is precise reproducible and technically simple with a high fusion rate and minimal donor site morbidity

the determination of urea kinetics plays a central role in clinical dialysis prescription there persist however significant limitations to current approaches particularly as they pertain to rigorous explorations of urea metabolism distribution and removal this report describes methodologies designed to address these limitations by coupling a SD nitrogen isotope method with strict compartmental pharmacokinetic modeling the findings of the present T0 can be summarized as follows first the use of SD isotope labeled exogenous urea is a reliable clinically applicable method for determination of urea kinetics second this method offers significant advantages in that it allows for an accurate measurement of urea DS endogenous urea production and nonrenal clearance of urea third this method is significantly more rigorous than urea kinetic models that utilize only endogenous urea and do not carefully fit data points finally pharmacokinetic modeling suggests that a twocompartment MM satisfies all aspects of urea CSD and removal but these compartments should not be equated with specific physiologic spaces the combination of stable isotope urea compartmental modeling is a rigorous methodology for the assessment and validation of urea kinetics

mutations in the GBA gba are associated with gauchers disease the most common lysosomal storage disorder parkinsonism is an established feature of gauchers disease and an increased frequency of mutations in gba has been reported in several different ethnic series with sporadic FPD in this study we evaluated the frequency of gba mutations in british patients affected by FPD we utilized the dna of patients and controls matched for age and ethnicity to screen for mutations within the gba gene clinical data on all identified gba mutation carriers was reviewed and analysed additionally in all cases where BB material was available a neuropathological DUE was performed and compared to sporadic FPD without gba mutations the frequency of gba mutations among the british patients was significantly higher p odds ratio confidence interval when compared to the control group fourteen different gba mutations were identified including three previously undescribed mutations ke dn and ge pathological examination revealed widespread and abundant AS pathology in all gba mutation carriers which were graded as braak stage of and had mckeiths limbic or diffuse neocortical lewy bodytype pathology diffuse neocortical lewy bodytype pathology tended to occur more frequently in the group with gba mutations compared to matched parkinsons disease controls clinical features comprised an EO of the disease the presence of hallucinations in and symptoms of cognitive decline or dementia in of patients this study demonstrates that gba mutations are found in british subjects at a higher frequency than any other known parkinsons disease gene this is the largest study to date on a nonjewish patient sample with a detailed genotypephenotypepathological analyses which strengthens the hypothesis that gba mutations represent a significant risk factor for the OD of parkinsons disease and suggest that to date this is the most common genetic factor identified for the disease

anthropogenic MICs may generate significant changes in the integrity of aquatic ecosystems so LT monitoring of populations that inhabit them is crucial counting micronucleated erythrocytes mn and ENA ena in IP blood is a widely used method for detecting chromosomal damage due to chemical agents in the water we analyzed mn and ena frequency in blood obtained from the common toad rhinella arenarum populations in sites with different degrees of environmental degradation the results of this T0 indicate that there is an association between the frequency of micronuclei and nuclear CA and the degree of environmental alteration recorded for the sites studied

the automatic analysis of subtle changes between mri scans is an important tool for assessing disease evolution over time manual labeling of evolutions in d data sets is tedious and error prone automatic NC detection however remains a challenging image processing problem a variety of mri artifacts introduce a wide range of unrepresentative changes between images making standard change detection methods unreliable in this study we describe an automatic image processing system that addresses these issues registration errors and undesired anatomical deformations are compensated using a versatile multiresolution deformable image matching method that preserves significant changes at a given scale a nonlinear intensity normalization method is associated with statistical hypothesis test methods to provide reliable NC PCD multimodal data is optionally exploited to reduce the false DR the performance of the system was evaluated on a large DB of d multimodal mr images of patients suffering from relapsing remitting multiple sclerosis ms the method was assessed using ROCs roc analysis and validated in a protocol involving two neurologists the automatic system outperforms the human expert detecting many lesion evolutions that are missed by the expert including small subtle changes

the influence of orotic acid on the incorporation of precursors into nucleic acids was studied in mice and rats and in isolated cells in vivo orotate levels were modified by two diets which are known to increase the rate of pyrimidine nucleotide synthesis in rat CL of these diets a orotate diet had greater inhibitory effects than an argininedeficient diet on the incorporation of horotate into rna of mouse kidney than mouse CL this contrasted with the situation in the rat where there was a greater effect in the CL than the kidney the situation in the rat was more readily interpreted than in the mouse in terms of previously established effects of these diets on ribonucleotide pool sizes however studies using hadenosine as a precursor for incorporation into rna suggested that even in the mouse the effects of orotate were on pool sizes rather than an inhibitory effect on rna synthesis the incorporation of hthymidine into dna was inhibited by orotate to a similar degree in cultured htc hepatoma cells and a line of RLE cells an effect on dna synthesis rather than solely on pool sizes was suggested by the observation that the pool size of dttp was not increased by mm orotate under conditions in which there was a fourfold increase in the level of utp in htc cells an inhibitory effect of orotate on dna synthesis was further supported by an observation of decreased incorporation of hdeoxyadenosine into dna and a lower rate of cellular proliferation

quantitative ultrastructural analysis revealed that the C1 densecored vesicles in the axon terminals in the myenteric ganglia of the guineapig stomach tend to be concentrated towards the ends of the terminals in guineapigs treated with mgkg hydroxydopamine diameters of the LDV were increased and their CSD in the terminals was altered treatment with reserpine prior to hydroxydopamine increased the proportion of large DCVs and decreased the overall number of LDV in the terminals the diameter of the LDV was increased both in the terminal profiles and in the nonterminal segments of the axons these observations are consistent with the view that uptake of hydroxydopamine into small vesicles is followed by their transformation into large DCVs

the relationship between personality traits and cognitive performance was studied in two CG men with symptoms and NP test results compatible with TE te and demographically similar healthy men n per group personality traits were assessed with the karolinska scales of personality ksp the neuropsychological examination included tests covering various PET domains the te group displayed elevated scores on all three ksp anxiety scales as well as an elevated impulsiveness score furthermore the te group had a lower score on the socialization scale than did the referent group different relationships between personality dispositions and cognitive SF-36 emerged in the two groups within the referent group the highest correlations were observed between ksp anxiety and socialization scale scores and reaction times measures this pattern did not appear in the te group instead divergent and a few weak relationships emerged these relationships involved correlations between the ksp monotony avoidance score and some motor speed scores by dividing the referent group into low anxiety and high anxiety subgroups on the basis of the multicomponent anxiety scale score it was shown that the test scores in the high anxiety subgroup mostly were indistinguishable from the scores in the te group in contrast the low anxiety group had higher test scores than the te group in of the tests in conclusion the expected relationship between anxiety and cognitive vigilance is absent in te cases this indicates that the NP performance decrement in te cases is not primarily related to elevated mental distress but is probably dominated by the effects of organic BB impairment thus in te cases low NP test scores should not be regarded as a consequence of emotional symptoms furthermore personality traits may be considered as potential confounders even if traditional matching by demographic criteria has been successfully implemented

interaction of the tn encoded tetr with the tet operator is studied by thermal denaturation of the specific complexes employing operator containing purified dna TRF varying in length from bp to bp comparison of the melting curves obtained with the free dna and dnarepressor complexes revealed a TPS stabilisation of the operator containing cooperatively melting segment in multiphasic denaturation curves under limiting concentrations of tetr the denaturation of the free dna is observed next to the denaturation of the repressordna complex quantitative analysis yields a binding curve with a stoichiometry of four tet repressors per tet operator containing fragment the Td of the complex is almost independent of the ionic strength indicating that the protein component denatures at this temperature the t1/2 time of the tet repressortet operator complex is greater than min under these conditions the tet operator on the bp fragment is determined to be located between a xba i and a sau a site by removing base pairs from either end of the fragment and subsequent comparison of the melting curves it is concluded that the tet repressor recognizes the double stranded rather than a possible cruciform structure of the tet operator the influence of a RII protein on the thermal stability of a genetic control region is discussed with respect to its possible influence on the initiation of transcription

the PPG ppg waveform also known as the pulse oximeter waveform is one of the most commonly displayed clinical waveforms first described in the s the technology behind the waveform is simple the waveform as displayed on the modern PP oximeter is an amplified and highly filtered measurement of CS absorption by the local tissue over time it is optimized by medical device manufacturers to accentuate its pulsatile components physiologically it is the result of a complex and not well understood interaction between the cardiovascular respiratory and autonomic systems all modern pulse oximeters extract and display the heart rate and oxygen saturation derived from the ppg measurements at multiple wavelengths as is the ppg is an excellent monitor for cardiac arrhythmia particularly when used in conjunction with the electrocardiogram ecg with slight modifications in the display of the ppg either to a strip chart recorder or slowed down on the monitor screen the ppg can be used to measure the ventilatorinduced modulations which have been associated with hypovolemia research efforts are under way to analyze the ppg using improved digital signal processing methods to develop new physiologic parameters it is hoped that when these new physiologic parameters are combined with a more modern understanding of cardiovascular physiology functional hemodynamics the potential utility of the ppg will be expanded the clinical researchers objective is the use of the ppg to guide early goaldirected therapeutic interventions fluid vasopressors and inotropes in effect to extract from the simple ppg the information and therapeutic guidance that was previously only obtainable from an arterial pressure line and the pulmonary i.a. catheter

VNS vns was approved by the us fda in as an adjunctive treatment for medically refractory epilepsy it is considered for use in patients who are poor candidates for resection or those in whom resection has failed however disagreement regarding the utility of vns in epilepsy continues because of the variability in benefit reported across clinical studies moreover although vns was approved only for adults and adolescents with partial epilepsy its tau in children and in patients with generalized epilepsy remains unclear the authors performed the first metaanalysis of vns tau in epilepsy identifying clinical studies with patients suffering from intractable epilepsy these studies included blinded RCTs class i evidence nonblinded randomized controlled trials class ii evidence prospective studies class iii evidence and numerous retrospective studietion average inhospital mortality was in trials using both thoracotomy and nonthoracotomy icds in trials using nonthoracotomy icds and in crt trials the PTX rate was similar between the nonthoracotomy icd and crt trials coronary sinus complications occurred in of patients undergoing crt lead dislodgement rates were higher in crt trials than in nonthoracotomy icd trials

an inflammatory bowel disease ibd comparable to human ulcerative colitis is induced upon transfer of t celldepleted wildtype f bone marrow into syngeneic t celldeficient tg epsilon mice f tg epsilon previously we have shown that activated cd t cells predominate in transplanted tg epsilon mice and adoptive transfer experiments verified the potential of these cells to cause disease in immunodeficient recipient mice using flow cytometry for the PCD of intracellular cytokine expression we demonstrate in the present T0 that large numbers of cd and cd tcr alphabeta TA from the IEL region and lamina propria of the colon of diseased but not from diseasetion led to suspect cushings syndrome screening tests UFC and mg dexamethasone were inconclusive but a detailed endocrine work up confirmed the presence of acthdependent hypercortisolism the patient was cured by the removal of a acthsecreting microadenoma by transsphenoidal route the present case provides a clinical demonstration of a previous exp evidence that a hypercoagulable state is present in cushings syndrome

the bioactive signaling lipid sphingosinephosphate sp and its analog fingolimod fty have previously shown neuroprotective effects against ischemic brain injury however the underlying mechanisms have not yet been fully clarified the roles of autophagy in IS are being increasingly recognized in the present study we sought to determine whether the sp pathway is involved in neuronal autophagy and investigate its possible mechanisms following stroke interestingly we found that fty significantly attenuates infarct volumes and reduces neuronal apoptosis on days and post stroke accompanied by amelioration of functional deficits additionally fty was found to decrease the induction of autophagosome proteins microtubuleassociated protein light chain lcii and beclin following IS in a dosedependent manner meanwhile protein C2 of the rat target of rapamycin mtor and the kda ribosomal protein s kinase psk were also upregulated in ftytreated animals and the nonspecific sphk inhibitor DMS dms was found to cause a reverse effect our results indicate that modulation of the sp signaling pathway by fty could effectively decrease neuronal autophagy through the mtorpsk pathway and attenuate ICM brain injury in mice

the proteoglycans synthesized by fibroblasts derived from healthy human adult dental pulps have been isolated and characterized on the basis of their glycosaminoglycan content molecular size and charge the proteoglycans were identified by their labelling with s sulphate and susceptibility to digestion by papain the sulphated glycosaminoglycans associated with the proteoglycans were identified following specific enzymatic and chemical degradations as CS DS and heparan sulphate DS and chondroitin sulphate and HS were the principal glycosaminoglycans associated with the cell layers the proteoglycans could be fractionated on the basis of their charge and size into a number of heterogeneous pools the principal proteoglycans isolated were small and contained either chondroitin sulphate or dermatan sulphate and most likely correspond to decorin and biglycan other molecules with features similar to versican and syndecan were also identified

testosterones t antiseizure effects may be mediated in part by actions of its alphareduced metabolites to test this hypothesis t was po to knockout mice deficient in the alphareductase type i enzyme and wildtype controls and their ictal activity following PTZ ptz mgkg ip was compared to mice administered vehicle t to wildtype mice increased latencies to forelimb clonus tonic clonic seizures hindlimb extension and death compared to that seen with vehicle administration moreover incidence of tonic clonic seizures and hindlimb extension were reduced in WT po t compared to vehicleadministered mice t administration to WT reduced ictal activity compared to t to KO mice which were not different than vehicleadministered control mice t to wildtype mice increased the latencies and decreased the incidence of forelimb clonus compared to t to KO mice which were not different from vehicleadministered mice these data are consistent with t having anticonvulsant effects and that alphareduced metabolites may mitigate some of ts antiseizure effects

more than million people in the united states suffer from severe headaches most have been diagnosed as migraines which have been assumed to be an intracranial process recognition of the extracranial sources of headaches such as supraorbital neuralgia infraorbital neuralgia auriculotemporal neuralgia facial neuralgia posterior auricular neuralgia occipital neuralgia cervical facet pathology masseter spasm SCM muscle spasm TR spasm and interspinous ligament pathology has led to an expansion of the treatment options available for practitioners skilled in interventional pain procedures however unless the clinical presentation is recognized treatment cannot be offered clinical presentation diagnostic injections differential diagnosis and T3 neurolytic techniques are discussed in this article

a simple polysaccharide the neutral mannan from saccharomyces cerevisiae wild type strain wnm was found to kill ddy CS mice by intravenous administration showing a ld value of mgkg on the other hand the acidic mannan fraction from the same yeast containing phosphate wam and chemically phosphorylated wnm wnmp were practically nontoxic concerning the relationship between chemical structure and lethal effect of these mannans it was demonstrated that a mannan possessing a highly branched structure exhibited stronger lethality than those with less branched structures against C3 strain mice with no responsiveness to lipopolysaccharide the ld value of wnm was as high as mgkg pretreatment with mgkg of dmannose nacetyldglucosamine dgalactose and lfucose prevented mice from the lethal effects of wnm however wnm ld did not show any lethal effect in mice for to hr after treatment with dexamethasone an antiinflammatory steroid

prion diseases are rare neurodegenerative disorders that always lead to death and that can be transmissible under certain conditions although sporadic creutzfeldtjakobs disease cjd is the best known human variant of these transmissible spongiform encephalopathies with an incidence of about in inhabitants several other types of human prion disease have been described eg familial cjd gerstmannstrÃ¤usslerscheinker syndrome fatal familial insomnia in a variant of cjd has been linked to the epidemic of BSE bse therefore vigilance concerning prion diseases was increased throughout the whole of europe in belgium a comprehensive nationwide T0 has been conducted both retrospectively and prospectively to identify prion disease patients in a surveillance system has also been created to monitor the incidence of cjd and other prion diseases using data from both studies and the surveillance program the occurrence and phenotype of all types of prion diseases in belgium was investigated the sporadic type of cjd was identified in patients while suffered from a hereditary form in our series we could find no evidence for variant or iatrogenic cjd neither for the more rare types of prion diseases

biopolymer based hydrogel materials are attractive options for a variety of medical applications including drug delivery and tissue regeneration because of their innate biomimetic material properties while biopolymers are typically selected for a TPS application based off of their chemical properties the overall material structure of the resulting hydrogel ultimately relates to its ability to function for its intended application in view of this it is imperative that the impact of commonly used drying procedures on hydrogel PCS properties is well understood herein the effects of postsynthesis drying techniques airdrying and lyophilization on genipin crosslinked chitosan hydrogel physical structure were studied chitosangenipin hydrogels synthesized out of either kda mw or kda mw chitosan were either airdried ad flashfrozen ff and then lyophilized or stepdown frozen sd and then lyophilized ESEM esem was employed to evaluate the resulting hydrogels PCS structure as a function of chitosan molecular weight and drying condition esem images revealed the presence of larger microscale pores within the sd samples compared to ff samples but both treatments yielded the induction of micropore with sizes ranging between Î¼m in diameter into the hydrogels traditional hydrogel swelling studies were performed to assess the resulting hydrogels swelling profile as a function of chitosan molecular weight and drying treatment lyophilized hydrogels showed a fivefold increase in swelling ratio compared to ad hydrogels indicating a change in morphology due to the drying process the results demonstrate that regardless of CP molecular weight postprocessing technique had a strong rho with hydrogel porosity

several lines of evidence suggest that the dysregulation of the immune system is an important factor in the OD of depression microglia are the resident macrophages of the CNS system and a key player in innate immunity of the brain we hypothesized that PS an rat of depression as a priming factor could affect microglial cells and might lead to depressivelike disturbances in AD male rat F1 we investigated the behavioral changes sucrose preference test porsolt test the expression of cq and cd mrna and the level of microglia iba positive in monthold control and prenatally stressed male F1 rats in addition we characterized the morphological and PSA parameters of potentially harmful no inos ilÎ² il il tnfÎ± ccl cxcl ccr X4 and beneficial insulinlike growth factor igf BB derived neurotrophic factor bdnf phenotypes in cultures of microglia obtained from the cortices of days old control and PS pups the adult PS rats showed behavioral anhedonic and depressionlike disturbances enhanced expression of microglial activation markers and an increased number of ibaimmunopositive cells in the hippocampus and FC cortex the morphology of glia was altered in cultures from PS rats as demonstrated by immunofluorescence microscopy moreover in these cultures we observed enhanced expression of cd and mhc ii and PR of Th1 cytokines including ilÎ² il tnfÎ± and il PS significantly upregulated C2 of the chemokines ccl cxcl and altered expression of their receptors ccr and X4 while igf production was suppressed in cultures of microglia from PS rats our results suggest that PS may lead to excessive microglia activation and contribute to the behavioral changes observed in depression in adulthood

seventythree diabetics males and females who complained of symptoms suggestive of autonomic neuropathy were followed prospectively for up to five years thirty patients presented with impotence CT while the other presented with one or more of the following postural hypotension intermittent diarrhoea hypoglycaemic PCU sweating CA and gastric fullness most subjects with impotence alone had normal autonomic function tests responses to the valsalva manoeuvre and sustained handgrip whereas the majority with other symptoms had abnormal tests twentysix subjects males and six females died during the followup period of the with initially normal autonomic CF tests five per cent died whereas of the with initially abnormal tests per cent died diabetics with symptoms of autonomic neuropathy and abnormal autonomic function tests had a calculated mortality rate T3 twoandahalf years of per cent and T3 five years of per cent half the deaths in those with abnormal tests were from renal failure and the remainder were either sudden and unexpected or from other causes which may have been associated with the autonomic neuropathy autonomic function testing repeated during the followup period showed that some normal tests later became abnormal but once tests were abnormal they usually remained abnormal a number of subjects with impotence CT developed other features of AN and abnormal tests during the follow up period symptoms of autonomic neuropathy particularly postural SH gastric symptoms and hypoglycaemic unawareness together with abnormal autonomic CF tests carry a very poor prognosis diarrhoea and importence on their own cannot be relied on as symptoms of AN autonomic CF testing using simple cardiovascular reflexes give a good guide to the prognosis of DAN

starting with the year a total pilot units have been organized in as many counties in which a complex program of surveillance of streptococcal infections was applied that had the following objectives prevention and fight against acute streptococcal infection prevention of severe complications of ARF of rheumatic carditis and acute glomerulonephritis prevention of relapses of ARF the establishment and verification of methods for clinical and laboratory diagnosis was followed as well as of therapeutic techniques and dispensarization the analysis of results obtained after the application for a period of years of the program in highrisk collectivities and in the conditions of an intensive circulation of some epidemic strains of streptococcus has shown the decrease each year of the incidence of scarlet fever the increased efficiency of detection of acute noneruptive nonrash streptococcal infections as well as a significant decrease in the number of cases that developed a first attack of ARF and acute glomerulonephritis a significant decrease was also obtained in the number of relapses and of severe evolution of rheumatic carditis due to active PCD continuous evidence prophylaxis and periodic control of cases with acute rheumatic fever

in eight patients with PDR treated with focal photocoagulation for preretinal new vessels recurrent preretinal newvessel systems supplied from the choroid developed subsequent treatment in most cases failed to produce permanent obliteration of the newvessels networks

melaninconcentrating hormone is a NPY produced in teleost hypothalami and transferred to the NH salmon mch was a novel cyclic heptadecapeptide capable of inducing melanin aggregation of integumentary melanophores at picoto nanomolar concentrations in all teleosts tested the mch gene is intronless and the exon encodes a amino acid precursor protein in which the heptadecapeptide of mch locates at the cterminal end immunohistochemical surveys with antisalmon mch AS strongly suggest that an mchlike peptide is present in the hypothalami of higher vertebrates biological effects of salmon mch on other vertebrates are found to be versatile

the preparation and performance of an epoxybased matrix impregnated with pyrromethene for solidstate dye laser applications are discussed the matrix proved to be SD and efficient as a laser medium when pumped by a frequencydoubled qswitched ndyag laser with a ns PW stability measurements were performed on a mmthick epoxy sample doped with pyrromethene at a concentration of x m when the sample was pumped at millijoule energy levels the stability was measured to be pulses from a single spot on the sample before the SP dropped by a factor of half

in the presence of phospholipid LDV and calcium ions PZ pz serves as a cofactor for the inhibition of coagulation factor xa by a plasma protein called pzdependent KPI zpi to further characterize zpi its cdna has been isolated and cloned from a human CL cdna library the zpi cdna is kb in length and has a relatively long region nt that contains six potential atg translation start codons atgs are followed by short open reading frames whereas atg and atg are in an uninterrupted open reading frame that includes the encoded zpi protein in vitro experiments show that atg is sufficient for the expression of rzpi in cultured CHO-K1 RNA analysis suggests the liver is a major site of zpi synthesis the predicted residue CAA CS of the mature zpi protein is rat with members of the serpin superfamily of protease inhibitors and is identical to the amino acid CS predicted by a previously described cdna isolated from rat CL regenerationassociated serpin protein rasp thus zpi is likely the human homologue of rat rasp alignment of the CAA CS of zpi with those of other serpins predicts that y is the p residue at the reactive center of the zpi molecule consistent with this notion rzpiya an altered form of zpi in which tyrosine has been changed to alanine lacks pzdependent factor xa inhibitory activity
